user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
if this 6850 call has a low implied volatility, that says the market does not expect a too wide range for this call.
and bcs volatility is not just a range but also time (velocity!!!!),  
this basically translates into: market does not expect a high chance for this call to end up with any intrinsic value (end up ITM)

user.name: Alma

comment.body:
if the implied volatility would suddenly catch up on this OTM call, that would signal that the market expectations has shifted, and the market suddenly started to expect a wider range for the call, 
hence the probability to finish ITM has grown
comment.raw_body:

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
why? 
bcs volatility is SD.
and every SD or sigma has an assigned probability.
one sigma move has 68.2% for example, adn so on...

so if the one sigma move is wider than what the call would need to be ATM, the probability to finish ITM will grown up to 68.2% and more

comment.body:
oh, one thing here:
don't be confused by statements like "0DTEs have no vega".

both is true.

0DTEs' vega is very small, market makers does not hedge it.
they hedge 0DTE gamma.

but if 0DTE would not have vega, then premium would not change to IV changes
comment.raw_body:
